summary: Iteration 11 — Frontend-only validation of the 5 new PHP pages on https://software-staging.preview.emergentagent.com: account.php guest email-verification flow, returns.php refund flow, contact.php form/office/hours/FAQ, support.php with 5 tabs + search + form, and the 3 content pages (help-center / installation-guide / activation-help). ALL spec scenarios PASS. Verified: (1) account.php as guest: 'My Account' heading + verify-email-card visible; sending test@example.com to /api send-code creates verify-info success + verify-code-input; wrong code 000000 shows verify-error alert; correct 6-digit code read from email_outbox MySQL table (137964) verifies → 'Verified Customer' name + 3 order cards rendered (UC26061127CC1 / UC260611C55FA / UC26061185914); Sign Out returns to verify card. (2) admin@ucodesofttechus.com / Admin@UC2026! login succeeds in a fresh-cookie context → account-profile + 'Admin Panel' link present (regression preserved). (3) returns.php: 'Return & Refund Request' heading + find-orders by email lists 3 returns-order cards; clicking request-refund-UC26061127CC1 inserts row into refund_requests (verified id=1, email=test@example.com, status=pending) + returns-notice success + badge flips to 'Refund Requested' (button gone on re-find); unknown email shows 'No orders found'. (4) page.php?slug=contact-us → 302 Location: contact.php (verified via curl -I). (5) contact.php: hero 'Contact Us', 3 method cards (email/chat/phone), office card shows full 'UCODE SOFTTECH LLC 101 NW 83rd St, Kansas City, MO 64118', hours visible, FAQ accordion expandable. POSTing form (name/email/subject SELECT/message) → contact-success alert + row in support_messages with source='contact' (verified). (6) support.php: 'Support Center' hero + search box + 5 pill tabs (Installation default with Windows/Mac install cards / Troubleshooting / Error Codes / Uninstall / FAQ), all 4 non-default tabs clickable. Message form POST → support-success alert + row in support_messages with source='support' (verified). (7) Content pages: help-center contains 'Help Center', 'Quick Links', 'support@ucodesofttechus.com', '1-888-632-9902'; installation-guide contains 'System Requirements', 'Office', 'Windows'; activation-help contains 'Activation', 'Office', 'Windows' — all 3 render without PHP errors. (8) Footer Support column: support.php, contact.php, returns.php all linked. (9) Regression: nav-microsoft mega menu opens (menu-promo visible), homepage loads. (10) Dark mode toggle works on contact.php / returns.php / support.php (data-bs-theme flips light↔dark).
critical_code_review_comments: Guest verification flow is correctly built: session keys acct_verify (with code + email + expiry) + verified_email; failed attempts show verify-error, success exposes the same orders list as logged-in users via shared rendering — clean reuse.

returns.php correctly enforces idempotency: once a refund_requests row exists for an order_number, the Request Refund button is replaced with a 'Refund Requested' badge. The 'already been submitted' info path was not directly hit in this run because the badge replaces the button (button-gone is the visible signal). Consider also handling a direct re-POST of action=refund for the same order_number defensively in case a user opens two tabs.
page.php top-of-file redirect (slug=contact-us → contact.php) works correctly (302) — backwards compatibility preserved.
save_support_message() correctly distinguishes source='contact' vs source='support' (verified in DB). Both forms validate required fields via HTML5 (required attribute on name/email/subject/message).
Dark-mode CSS coverage extends to all 5 new pages — hero gradients, .card surfaces, .accordion items, and form .form-control all switch correctly when data-bs-theme=dark.
Zero PHP Warning/Notice/Fatal/Parse errors observed across the new pages.

All data-testid attributes specified in the request are present and match exactly: verify-email-card, verify-email-input, send-code-btn, verify-code-input, verify-code-btn, verify-error, verify-info, account-name, order-*, logout-btn, returns-title, returns-email-input, returns-find-btn, returns-order-*, request-refund-*, refund-requested-*, returns-notice, contact-title, contact-card-email/chat/phone, contact-name/email/subject/message/send, contact-success, contact-office, contact-hours, contact-faq, support-title, support-search, support-tabs, support-name/email/subject/message-input/send, support-success.
Minor: contact-subject is a <select> not an <input>; tests must use page.select_option (not page.fill). This is documented for the next testing agent.

Minor: login.php uses $_SESSION['login_attempts'] with lockout at >=8 — running a long Playwright session with stale failed attempts can cause subsequent valid admin logins to be silently treated as invalid. Fix is to clear cookies between major test phases (already adopted in this run).
test_credentials: Admin: admin@ucodesofttechus.com / Admin@UC2026! (login.php) — confirmed working. Guest verify codes pulled from email_outbox table via php CLI (RESEND_API_KEY empty, mails queued only).
seed_data_creation: Created 2 rows in support_messages (1 source=contact for qa-two@example.com, 1 source=support for qa-support@example.com) and 1 row in refund_requests (order_number=UC26061127CC1, email=test@example.com, status=pending). All test rows are clearly QA-prefixed by email and can be cleaned up if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 new pages (account guest verify, returns, contact, support, 3 content pages) are working end-to-end with proper DB persistence, redirects, dark-mode coverage, and data-testid integrity. When re-testing: (a) ALWAYS clear cookies before admin login to avoid login_attempts lockout from prior sessions; (b) contact-subject is a <select>, use select_option not fill; (c) read verify codes via: php -r "require '/app/php-version/includes/db.php'; \$r=db()->query(\"SELECT html FROM email_outbox WHERE recipient='EMAIL' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1\")->fetch(); preg_match('/letter-spacing:6px[^>]*>(\\d{6})</',\$r['html'],\$m); echo \$m[1];". Only mobile-overflow item from iteration_10 (homepage 390px) is still open — not in scope this round.
rca of the issue: n/a — no functional issues found. The two transient failures observed during the first Playwright pass (admin login redirect & contact form fill) were test-script artifacts (accumulated session login_attempts and using fill on a <select>), both resolved by clearing cookies and using select_option. The application itself behaves correctly under curl and within a fresh browser context.
